# 10S Workflows - Audio Driven v5

These are advanced quality-focused conditioned workflows for 10Eros/Sulphur/Base LTX 2.3. They make some compromises on speed for quality. 
Steal these or study 10S Node intergation if you are a workflow maker or want to customize and release them as your own.  
**They are NOT FAST.** I find with LTX, fast ≠ good.

---

## 📦 Downloads

| Resource | Link |
|----------|------|
| 🎬 Workflows | [LTX2.3-10Eros Workflows](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3-10Eros_Workflows) |
| 🧠 Models (10Eros/Sulphur) | [huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3-10Eros](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3-10Eros) |
| 🔧 10S Comfy Nodes | [TenStrip/10S-Comfy-nodes](https://github.com/TenStrip/10S-Comfy-nodes) |
| ⚗️ Distilled LoRAs | [LTX2.3 Distilled LoRA 1.1 Experiments](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3_Distilled_Lora_1.1_Experiments/tree/main) |
| 🌀 Echo LoRA | [JoyAI Echo Extract](https://huggingface.co/TenStrip/LTX2.3_JoyAI_Lora_Extracted/tree/main) |
| 🔊 Audio Reactive LoRA | [LTX2.3 Audio Reactive LoRA](https://huggingface.co/fal/ltx2.3-audio-reactive-lora/tree/main) |
---

## ⚙️ How It Works

These workflows rely on **[10S-Comfy-nodes]**

Mouse over parameters for info.


For Landscape ratios and formats **bypass tiling** on Tiled Sampler or increase to 3 tiles for smaller fine motion detail, but it is not necessary to correct that ratio.

Distillation is handled at full break down. Lower pass video distillation will increase motion. Upscale pass video distillation will add detail, but also add artifacts and unaesthetic blur artifacts if too high. Audio is provided only by uploaded audio clips in this workflow.

The first pass output is a full **motion** preview, if it contains mutation, distortion, bad motion, etc... The upscale pass will probably not fix it. Only allow it to run to upscale when first pass motion looks good. **Visual quality and style will be restored** at upscale.


---

## ✍️ Prompt Enhancement

These use STG Guider and have CFG for initial steps which is where most broad motion and audio form. **Your negative prompt is impactful, so use it.**

For best positive prompt results, use this foreword in **Grok** or an **uncensored LLM**:

You are generating a video scene script based on the attached image. Use this exact format and method:

- Output ONLY plain English text inside a single markdown code block.
- Use 4-second timestamp blocks in this exact style: ([0-4 sec]), ([4-8 sec]), ([8-12 sec]).
- First block ([0-4 sec]) briefly describes the initial scene from the image (subject, pose, clothing, setting).
- All following blocks focus heavily on all movements and evolutions in the scene.
- The user will provide a central idea/action. Center the entire script around that idea and make it the main focus throughout all time blocks.
- Describe the movements in detail, frame by frame within each 4-second block.
- Keep the language concise, direct, and movement-focused. No unnecessary embellishment or long descriptions.
- If the central idea involves complex sequence of motion, break it down into flowing segments. Make that action continuous and prominent across all time blocks.
- End at the second mark per instruct.

Image is attached. 

Central idea: [user's specific idea here and length in seconds].

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
